caption: The image shows a group of people wearing aprons and gloves, actively handling salted napa cabbage and red chili paste (gochugaru) on large tables, which aligns with the communal kimchi-making practice known as Kimjang.
The image shows a person wearing an apron and gloves, handling salted napa cabbage and red chili paste, which aligns with the communal kimchi-making practice known as Kimjang.
The image shows people wearing traditional white hanbok and waving the Taegeukgi, which are characteristic of the Korean independence movement.
The image shows individuals wearing traditional Korean attire, including white hanbok, and one person holding a document, with the Taegeukgi (Korean flag) prominently displayed in the background.
The image features a person wearing a vibrant red hanbok adorned with floral patterns, which aligns with the visual characteristics of a Korean traditional wedding ceremony.

The image shows a couple dressed in colorful ceremonial hanbok, seated before a ritual table adorned with wooden mandarin ducks and candles, which are characteristic elements of a Korean traditional wedding ceremony.
The image shows a ritual table with neatly arranged food offerings, including fruits, pastries, and a teapot, which aligns with the visual characteristics of an Ancestral rite (Jesa).
The image shows a group of people performing deep bows in front of a ritual table adorned with neatly arranged food offerings, which is characteristic of a Korean ancestral memorial rite (Jesa).

The image shows a group of people standing in a line, facing forward, which aligns with the visual characterization of a Korean grave-tending rite, but there are no visible grass-covered burial mounds or food offerings in the image.

The image shows a group of people gathered around a grass-covered burial mound, with food offerings placed before the grave, which aligns with the description of Seongmyo, a Korean grave-tending rite.
The image shows a cylindrical stacked-stone tower with a square top frame, which matches the description of Cheomseongdae observatory, standing in an open field.
The image shows a cylindrical stacked-stone tower with a square top frame, standing in an open field, which matches the description of Cheomseongdae observatory.
The image showcases the wooden pavilion with upturned eaves and colorful dancheong paintwork of Gyeongbokgung Palace, illuminated against a dark blue sky.
The image shows a wooden pavilion with upturned eaves and colorful dancheong paintwork, characteristic of Gyeongbokgung Palace.
The image shows a shallow waterway bordered by stone embankments and pedestrian walkways, which aligns with the description of Cheonggyecheon as an urban stream in Seoul.
The statue in the image depicts Admiral Yi Sun-shin wearing Joseon military armor and holding a sword, which aligns with the visual characterization of the Korean naval hero.
The image shows two individuals performing a Jeol and Sebae, characterized by their kneeling posture and deep bows with hands on the floor, dressed in black formal attire.
The image shows two children wearing hanbok, kneeling on the floor with their hands on the ground, which visually characterizes Jeol and Sebae, Korean formal deep bows.
The image shows two individuals wearing hanbok, one of whom is kneeling and bowing deeply with hands on the floor, which aligns with the visual characteristics of Jeol and Sebae.
The statue in the image depicts a seated figure wearing royal Joseon robes and a winged crown, with Hangul script visible on the pedestal.

The statue of Sejongdaewang, King Sejong the Great, is depicted as a seated figure in royal Joseon robes with a winged crown (ikseongwan), and the Hangul script "세종대왕" is prominently displayed on the pedestal.
The kite in the image has a colorful geometric pattern, which aligns with the description of Yeon-nalligi, a traditional Korean kite flying activity.
The image shows a person flying a kite with a colorful geometric pattern, which aligns with the description of Yeon-nalligi, a traditional Korean kite flying activity.
The image shows a group of people seated on the floor around a colorful mat with floral patterns, which is characteristic of the game board used for Yutnori, a traditional Korean board game.
The image shows two children wearing traditional Korean hanbok, sitting on the floor, with wooden sticks (yut) mid-air above them, indicating they are playing the traditional Korean game of Yutnori.
The image shows women in traditional Korean hanbok holding hands in a large circle formation outdoors, which is characteristic of the Ganggangsullae dance.
The image shows women in hanbok holding hands in a large circle formation on a stage, which is visually characteristic of the Ganggangsullae Korean circle dance.
The performer is wearing an exaggerated painted mask and a loose, colorful robe, which are characteristic features of the Talchum, a Korean masked dance-drama.
The image displays a variety of colorful, exaggerated painted masks, which are characteristic of Talchum, a Korean masked dance-drama.
The image shows a bowl of Ramyeon with yellow curly noodles and an orange-red broth, accompanied by chopsticks resting on the rim of the bowl.
The image shows a small pot containing yellow curly noodles in an orange-red broth, which aligns with the visual characteristics of Ramyeon as described.
The image shows cylindrical rolls of rice with colorful fillings, wrapped in dark-green seaweed, and sliced into round pieces, which aligns with the description of gimbap.
The image shows small round golden-brown patties coated with an egg batter, which matches the description of Wanja-jeon, Korean pan-fried meatballs.
The image displays traditional Korean confections (Hangwa) with small decorative pieces in pastel colors and pressed floral or geometric patterns, as seen in the arrangement of the pastries.
The image shows a box of Hangwa, traditional Korean confections, featuring small decorative pieces in pastel colors and pressed floral patterns, with a central flower decoration.
The image shows a bowl of Yaksik, featuring a dark-brown glossy mound of sticky glutinous rice with visible chestnuts and jujubes.
The image shows small, flower-shaped or round pastries with a dark golden-brown glossy glaze, which aligns with the description of Yakgwa.
The image shows a dish that appears to be Bulgogi, characterized by its glossy dark-brown glaze and thinly sliced meat, though it is not on a grill but rather in a pan with vegetables and broth.

The image shows a bowl of Tteokguk, featuring a clear broth with white oval sliced rice cakes (tteok) and crescent-shaped dumplings (mandu), garnished with green onions, red chili pepper, and other toppings.
The image shows a bowl of soup with a clear broth, white oval sliced rice cakes (tteok), and crescent-shaped dumplings (mandu), which are characteristic features of Tteokguk and Mandu soup.
The image shows translucent brown noodles mixed with colorful julienned vegetables, giving it a glossy sesame-oil sheen, which are characteristic features of Japchae.

The image shows a traditional Korean building with a tiled roof and decorative elements, which aligns with the visual characteristics of Namhansanseong fortress, though it does not depict long stone walls or an arched gate.
The image shows wooden buildings with curved tile roofs and dancheong-painted eaves arranged around courtyards, consistent with the visual characteristics of a Korean temporary royal palace.
The image shows an arched stone gateway topped with a wooden pavilion, flanked by curved fortress walls, which are characteristic features of Paldalmun Gate at Hwaseong Fortress.
The image shows a tall white observation tower with a disc-shaped deck and a pointed spire, which matches the description of the EXPO Hanbit Tower.
The image depicts soldiers in military uniforms and vehicles, which aligns with the visual characteristics of the Korean War era.
The image depicts a scene of post-war devastation with damaged buildings and overgrown vegetation, which aligns with the visual characteristics of the Korean War era.

The image does not feature any soldier statues, memorial walls with engraved names, or wartime photographs; it depicts a serene village scene with traditional thatched-roof houses and people gathered near a body of water.

The image shows a family playing Jegichagi, where a small paper-and-coin shuttlecock is being kicked into the air with the foot, as depicted by the dynamic poses and the colorful shuttlecock in mid-air.
The image shows a small blue and white object in mid-air, resembling a shuttlecock, which aligns with the description of a jegi used in the Korean shuttlecock-kicking game Jegichagi.
